subject: Heads-up: MPI-130 (user-renamable cards) will touch MpiGalleryGrid + audio cards — small coordination needed
body:
New card MPI-130 lets users rename a gallery card via the card context menu. It adds a NEW field group.customName (string|null) on the ItemGroup in project.json itemGroups[]. The card name today is derived (MpiGalleryGrid.js:786 `selected?.name || group.name`); customName takes precedence: `customName || derived`.

WHY YOU CARE — it overlaps your audio work (Phase 4.5, landed today) in two places:

1) FILE OVERLAP — both cards edit js/components/Compounds/MpiGalleryGrid/MpiGalleryGrid.js. You added _swapThumbToAudio + _ensureAudioCardControls where AUDIO card-click = play/pause toggle. My rename adds (a) a context-menu 'Rename' item next to 'Card notes' (~L913) and (b) an inline MpiInput that swaps the `.mpi-group-card__name` span on the card. The inline edit will stopPropagation so typing/committing on an audio card does NOT trigger your audio play/pause — but flagging it so we don't write conflicting click handlers. I'll sequence MPI-130 AFTER your MpiGalleryGrid edits settle to avoid a parallel-edit collision.

2) AUDIO GROUPS ARE RENAMABLE TOO — audio cards are non-click-through (good), but they still need the context-menu Rename to work. Nothing for you to change there; just confirming customName is group-level so it covers image/video/audio groups uniformly.

ONE THING THAT MIGHT TOUCH YOUR CODE: if any of your audio code reads/writes the group object shape or persists groups, please use group.customName as the user-facing name (don't re-derive from filename) and don't strip unknown group keys on any reconcile/rebuild path you added. group.customName lives INLINE in project.json itemGroups[] (NOT a .meta sidecar — only history items get sidecars). If you didn't touch group persistence, ignore this.

No action required right now — this is a coordination heads-up. Reply if your audio card-click handler or any group-rebuild code would collide with a customName field, and I'll adjust MPI-130's sequencing.

recent_events.outcome: Coordination complete — green light to proceed, no collision.
